     6→// Package metricstore provides walCheckpoint.go: WAL-based checkpoint implementation.
     7→//
     8→// This replaces the Avro shadow tree with an append-only Write-Ahead Log (WAL)
     9→// per host, eliminating the extra memory overhead of the AvroStore and providing
    10→// truly continuous (per-write) crash safety.
    11→//
    12→// # Architecture
    13→//
    14→//	Metric write (DecodeLine)
    15→//	      │
    16→//	      ├─► WriteToLevel()    → main MemoryStore (unchanged)
    17→//	      │
    18→//	      └─► WALMessages channel
    19→//	               │
    20→//	               ▼
    21→//	        WALStaging goroutine
    22→//	               │
    23→//	               ▼
    24→//	        checkpoints/cluster/host/current.wal  (append-only, binary)
    25→//
    26→//	Periodic checkpoint (Checkpointing goroutine):
    27→//	  1. Write <timestamp>.bin snapshot (column-oriented, from main tree)
    28→//	  2. Signal WALStaging to truncate current.wal per host
    29→//
    30→//	On restart (FromCheckpoint):
    31→//	  1. Load most recent <timestamp>.bin snapshot
    32→//	  2. Replay current.wal (overwrite-safe: buffer.write handles duplicate timestamps)
    33→//
    34→// # WAL Record Format
    35→//
    36→//	[4B magic 0xCC1DA7A1][4B payload_len][payload][4B CRC32]
    37→//
    38→//	payload:
    39→//	  [8B timestamp int64]
    40→//	  [2B metric_name_len uint16][N metric name bytes]
    41→//	  [1B selector_count uint8]
    42→//	  per selector: [1B selector_len uint8][M selector bytes]
    43→//	  [4B value float32 bits]
    44→//
    45→// # Binary Snapshot Format
    46→//
    47→//	[4B magic 0xCC5B0001][8B from int64][8B to int64]
    48→//	Level tree (recursive):
    49→//	  [4B num_metrics uint32]
    50→//	  per metric:
    51→//	    [2B name_len uint16][N name bytes]
    52→//	    [8B frequency int64][8B start int64]
    53→//	    [4B num_values uint32][num_values × 4B float32]
    54→//	  [4B num_children uint32]
    55→//	  per child: [2B name_len uint16][N name bytes] + Level (recursive)
    56→package metricstore
    57→
    58→import (
    59→	"bufio"
    60→	"context"
    61→	"encoding/binary"
    62→	"fmt"
    63→	"hash/crc32"
    64→	"hash/fnv"
    65→	"io"
    66→	"math"
    67→	"os"
    68→	"path"
    69→	"strings"
    70→	"sync"
    71→	"sync/atomic"
    72→	"time"
    73→
    74→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    75→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    76→)
    77→
    78→// Magic numbers for binary formats.
    79→const (
    80→	walFileMagic   = uint32(0xCC1DA701) // WAL file header magic
    81→	walRecordMagic = uint32(0xCC1DA7A1) // WAL record magic
    82→	snapFileMagic  = uint32(0xCC5B0001) // Binary snapshot magic
    83→)
    84→
    85→// walShardChs holds per-shard channels for WAL messages.
    86→// Initialized by WALStaging; nil when WAL is not active.
    87→var walShardChs []chan *WALMessage
    88→
    89→// walShardRotateChs holds per-shard channels for WAL rotation requests.
    90→var walShardRotateChs []chan walRotateReq
    91→
    92→// walNumShards stores the number of shards (set during WALStaging init).
    93→var walNumShards int
    94→
    95→// walStagingWg tracks WALStaging goroutine exits for shutdown synchronization.
    96→var walStagingWg sync.WaitGroup
    97→
    98→// walShuttingDown is set before closing shard channels to prevent
    99→// SendWALMessage from sending on a closed channel (which panics in Go).
   100→var walShuttingDown atomic.Bool
   101→
   102→// walCheckpointActive is set during binary checkpoint writes.
   103→// While active, SendWALMessage skips sending (returns true) because the
   104→// snapshot captures all in-memory data, making WAL writes redundant.
   105→var walCheckpointActive atomic.Bool
   106→
   107→// WALMessage represents a single metric write to be appended to the WAL.
   108→// Cluster and Node are NOT stored in the WAL record (inferred from file path).
   109→type WALMessage struct {
   110→	MetricName string
   111→	Cluster    string
   112→	Node       string
   113→	Selector   []string
   114→	Value      schema.Float
   115→	Timestamp  int64
   116→}
   117→
   118→// walRotateReq requests WAL file rotation for a specific host directory.
   119→// The done channel is closed by the WAL goroutine when rotation is complete.
   120→type walRotateReq struct {
   121→	hostDir string
   122→	done    chan struct{}
   123→}
   124→
   125→// walFileState holds an open WAL file handle and buffered writer for one host directory.
   126→type walFileState struct {
   127→	f     *os.File
   128→	w     *bufio.Writer
   129→	dirty bool
   130→	size  int64 // approximate bytes written (tracked from open + writes)
   131→}
   132→
   133→// walFlushInterval controls how often dirty WAL files are flushed to disk.
   134→// Decoupling flushes from message processing lets the consumer run at memory
   135→// speed, amortizing syscall overhead across many writes.
   136→const walFlushInterval = 1 * time.Second
   137→
   138→// walShardIndex computes which shard a message belongs to based on cluster+node.
   139→// Uses FNV-1a hash for fast, well-distributed mapping.
   140→func walShardIndex(cluster, node string) int {
   141→	h := fnv.New32a()
   142→	h.Write([]byte(cluster))
   143→	h.Write([]byte{0})
   144→	h.Write([]byte(node))
   145→	return int(h.Sum32()) % walNumShards
   146→}
   147→
   148→// SendWALMessage routes a WAL message to the appropriate shard channel.
   149→// Returns false if the channel is full or shutdown is in progress.
   150→func SendWALMessage(msg *WALMessage) bool {
   151→	if walShardChs == nil || walShuttingDown.Load() {
   152→		return false
   153→	}
   154→	if walCheckpointActive.Load() {
   155→		return true // Data safe in memory; snapshot will capture it
   156→	}
   157→	shard := walShardIndex(msg.Cluster, msg.Node)
   158→	select {
   159→	case walShardChs[shard] <- msg:
   160→		return true
   161→	default:
   162→		return false
   163→	}
   164→}
   165→
   166→// WALStaging starts N sharded background goroutines that receive WALMessage items
   167→// and append binary WAL records to per-host current.wal files.
   168→// Each shard owns a subset of hosts (determined by hash of cluster+node),
   169→// parallelizing serialization and I/O while keeping per-host writes serial.
   170→func WALStaging(wg *sync.WaitGroup, ctx context.Context) {
   171→	if Keys.Checkpoints.FileFormat == "json" {
   172→		return
   173→	}
   174→
   175→	walNumShards = max(Keys.NumWorkers, 1)
   176→	chBufSize := max(65536/walNumShards, 1024)
   177→
   178→	walShardChs = make([]chan *WALMessage, walNumShards)
   179→	walShardRotateChs = make([]chan walRotateReq, walNumShards)
   180→
   181→	for i := range walNumShards {
   182→		walShardChs[i] = make(chan *WALMessage, chBufSize)
   183→		walShardRotateChs[i] = make(chan walRotateReq, 64)
   184→	}
   185→
   186→	for i := range walNumShards {
   187→		msgCh := walShardChs[i]
   188→		rotateCh := walShardRotateChs[i]
   189→
   190→		walStagingWg.Add(1)
   191→		wg.Go(func() {
   192→			defer walStagingWg.Done()
   193→			hostFiles := make(map[string]*walFileState)
   194→
   195→			defer func() {
   196→				for _, ws := range hostFiles {
   197→					if ws.f != nil {
   198→						ws.w.Flush()
   199→						ws.f.Close()
   200→					}
   201→				}
   202→			}()
   203→
   204→			getOrOpenWAL := func(hostDir string) *walFileState {
   205→				ws, ok := hostFiles[hostDir]
   206→				if ok {
   207→					return ws
   208→				}
   209→
   210→				if err := os.MkdirAll(hostDir, CheckpointDirPerms); err != nil {
   211→					cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: mkdir %s: %v", hostDir, err)
   212→					return nil
   213→				}
   214→
   215→				walPath := path.Join(hostDir, "current.wal")
   216→				f, err := os.OpenFile(walPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_APPEND|os.O_WRONLY, CheckpointFilePerms)
   217→				if err != nil {
   218→					cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: open %s: %v", walPath, err)
   219→					return nil
   220→				}
   221→
   222→				w := bufio.NewWriter(f)
   223→
   224→				// Write file header magic if file is new (empty).
   225→				info, err := f.Stat()
   226→				var fileSize int64
   227→				if err == nil {
   228→					fileSize = info.Size()
   229→				}
   230→				if err == nil && fileSize == 0 {
   231→					var hdr [4]byte
   232→					binary.LittleEndian.PutUint32(hdr[:], walFileMagic)
   233→					if _, err := w.Write(hdr[:]); err != nil {
   234→						cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: write header %s: %v", walPath, err)
   235→						f.Close()
   236→						return nil
   237→					}
   238→					fileSize = 4
   239→				}
   240→
   241→				ws = &walFileState{f: f, w: w, size: fileSize}
   242→				hostFiles[hostDir] = ws
   243→				return ws
   244→			}
   245→
   246→			processMsg := func(msg *WALMessage) {
   247→				hostDir := path.Join(Keys.Checkpoints.RootDir, msg.Cluster, msg.Node)
   248→				ws := getOrOpenWAL(hostDir)
   249→				if ws == nil {
   250→					return
   251→				}
   252→
   253→				// Enforce max WAL size: force-rotate before writing if limit is exceeded.
   254→				// The in-memory store still holds the data; only crash-recovery coverage is lost.
   255→				if maxSize := Keys.Checkpoints.MaxWALSize; maxSize > 0 && ws.size >= maxSize {
   256→					cclog.Warnf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: force-rotating %s (size %d >= limit %d)",
   257→						hostDir, ws.size, maxSize)
   258→					ws.w.Flush()
   259→					ws.f.Close()
   260→					walPath := path.Join(hostDir, "current.wal")
   261→					if err := os.Remove(walPath); err != nil && !os.IsNotExist(err) {
   262→						cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: remove %s: %v", walPath, err)
   263→					}
   264→					delete(hostFiles, hostDir)
   265→					ws = getOrOpenWAL(hostDir)
   266→					if ws == nil {
   267→						return
   268→					}
   269→				}
   270→
   271→				n, err := writeWALRecordDirect(ws.w, msg)
   272→				if err != nil {
   273→					cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: write record: %v", err)
   274→				}
   275→				ws.size += int64(n)
   276→				ws.dirty = true
   277→			}
   278→
   279→			processRotate := func(req walRotateReq) {
   280→				ws, ok := hostFiles[req.hostDir]
   281→				if ok && ws.f != nil {
   282→					ws.w.Flush()
   283→					ws.f.Close()
   284→					walPath := path.Join(req.hostDir, "current.wal")
   285→					if err := os.Remove(walPath); err != nil && !os.IsNotExist(err) {
   286→						cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: remove %s: %v", walPath, err)
   287→					}
   288→					delete(hostFiles, req.hostDir)
   289→				}
   290→				close(req.done)
   291→			}
   292→
   293→			flushDirty := func() {
   294→				for _, ws := range hostFiles {
   295→					if ws.dirty {
   296→						ws.w.Flush()
   297→						ws.dirty = false
   298→					}
   299→				}
   300→			}
   301→
   302→			ticker := time.NewTicker(walFlushInterval)
   303→			defer ticker.Stop()
   304→
   305→			// drainBatch processes up to 4096 pending messages without blocking.
   306→			// Returns false if the channel was closed.
   307→			drainBatch := func() bool {
   308→				for range 4096 {
   309→					select {
   310→					case msg, ok := <-msgCh:
   311→						if !ok {
   312→							flushDirty()
   313→							return false
   314→						}
   315→						processMsg(msg)
   316→					case req := <-rotateCh:
   317→						processRotate(req)
   318→					default:
   319→						return true
   320→					}
   321→				}
   322→				return true
   323→			}
   324→
   325→			for {
   326→				select {
   327→				case <-ctx.Done():
   328→					// On shutdown, skip draining buffered messages — a full binary
   329→					// checkpoint will be written from in-memory state, making
   330→					// buffered WAL records redundant.
   331→					flushDirty()
   332→					return
   333→				case msg, ok := <-msgCh:
   334→					if !ok {
   335→						return
   336→					}
   337→					processMsg(msg)
   338→					if !drainBatch() {
   339→						return
   340→					}
   341→					// No flush here — timer handles periodic flushing.
   342→				case <-ticker.C:
   343→					flushDirty()
   344→				case req := <-rotateCh:
   345→					processRotate(req)
   346→				}
   347→			}
   348→		})
   349→	}
   350→}
   351→
   352→// WaitForWALStagingDrain blocks until all WALStaging goroutines have exited.
   353→// Must be called after closing walShardChs to ensure all file handles are
   354→// flushed and closed before checkpoint writes begin.
   355→func WaitForWALStagingDrain() {
   356→	walStagingWg.Wait()
   357→}
   358→
   359→// RotateWALFiles sends rotation requests for the given host directories
   360→// and blocks until all rotations complete. Each request is routed to the
   361→// shard that owns the host directory.
   362→//
   363→// If shutdown is in progress (WAL staging goroutines may have exited),
   364→// rotation is skipped to avoid deadlocking on abandoned channels.
   365→func RotateWALFiles(hostDirs []string) {
   366→	if walShardRotateChs == nil || walShuttingDown.Load() {
   367→		return
   368→	}
   369→	deadline := time.After(2 * time.Minute)
   370→	dones := make([]chan struct{}, 0, len(hostDirs))
   371→	for _, dir := range hostDirs {
   372→		done := make(chan struct{})
   373→		shard := walShardIndexFromDir(dir)
   374→		select {
   375→		case walShardRotateChs[shard] <- walRotateReq{hostDir: dir, done: done}:
   376→			dones = append(dones, done)
   377→		case <-deadline:
   378→			cclog.Warnf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL rotation send timed out, %d of %d hosts remaining",
   379→				len(hostDirs)-len(dones), len(hostDirs))
   380→			goto waitDones
   381→		}
   382→	}
   383→waitDones:
   384→	for _, done := range dones {
   385→		select {
   386→		case <-done:
   387→		case <-time.After(30 * time.Second):
   388→			cclog.Warn("[METRICSTORE]> WAL rotation completion timed out, continuing")
   389→			return
   390→		}
   391→	}
   392→}
   393→
   394→// walShardIndexFromDir extracts cluster and node from a host directory path
   395→// and returns the shard index. The path format is: rootDir/cluster/node
   396→func walShardIndexFromDir(hostDir string) int {
   397→	// hostDir = .../cluster/node — extract last two path components
   398→	node := path.Base(hostDir)
   399→	cluster := path.Base(path.Dir(hostDir))
   400→	return walShardIndex(cluster, node)
   401→}
   402→
   403→// RotateWALFiles sends rotation requests for the given host directories
   404→// and blocks until all rotations complete.
   405→func RotateWALFilesAfterShutdown(hostDirs []string) {
   406→	for _, dir := range hostDirs {
   407→		walPath := path.Join(dir, "current.wal")
   408→		if err := os.Remove(walPath); err != nil && !os.IsNotExist(err) {
   409→			cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: remove %s: %v", walPath, err)
   410→		}
   411→	}
   412→}
   413→
   414→// writeWALRecordDirect encodes a WAL record into a contiguous buffer first,
   415→// then writes it to the bufio.Writer in a single call. This prevents partial
   416→// records in the write buffer if a write error occurs mid-record (e.g. disk full).
   417→// Returns the number of bytes written and any error.
   418→func writeWALRecordDirect(w *bufio.Writer, msg *WALMessage) (int, error) {
   419→	// Compute payload and total record size.
   420→	payloadSize := 8 + 2 + len(msg.MetricName) + 1 + 4
   421→	for _, s := range msg.Selector {
   422→		payloadSize += 1 + len(s)
   423→	}
   424→	// Total: 8 (header) + payload + 4 (CRC).
   425→	totalSize := 8 + payloadSize + 4
   426→
   427→	// Use stack buffer for typical small records, heap-allocate only for large ones.
   428→	var stackBuf [256]byte
   429→	var buf []byte
   430→	if totalSize <= len(stackBuf) {
   431→		buf = stackBuf[:totalSize]
   432→	} else {
   433→		buf = make([]byte, totalSize)
   434→	}
   435→
   436→	// Header: magic + payload length.
   437→	binary.LittleEndian.PutUint32(buf[0:4], walRecordMagic)
   438→	binary.LittleEndian.PutUint32(buf[4:8], uint32(payloadSize))
   439→
   440→	// Payload starts at offset 8.
   441→	p := 8
   442→
   443→	// Timestamp (8 bytes).
   444→	binary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(buf[p:p+8], uint64(msg.Timestamp))
   445→	p += 8
   446→
   447→	// Metric name length (2 bytes) + metric name.
   448→	binary.LittleEndian.PutUint16(buf[p:p+2], uint16(len(msg.MetricName)))
   449→	p += 2
   450→	p += copy(buf[p:], msg.MetricName)
   451→
   452→	// Selector count (1 byte).
   453→	buf[p] = byte(len(msg.Selector))
   454→	p++
   455→
   456→	// Selectors (1-byte length + bytes each).
   457→	for _, sel := range msg.Selector {
   458→		buf[p] = byte(len(sel))
   459→		p++
   460→		p += copy(buf[p:], sel)
   461→	}
   462→
   463→	// Value (4 bytes, float32 bits).
   464→	binary.LittleEndian.PutUint32(buf[p:p+4], math.Float32bits(float32(msg.Value)))
   465→	p += 4
   466→
   467→	// CRC32 over payload (bytes 8..8+payloadSize).
   468→	crc := crc32.ChecksumIEEE(buf[8 : 8+payloadSize])
   469→	binary.LittleEndian.PutUint32(buf[p:p+4], crc)
   470→
   471→	// Single atomic write to the buffered writer.
   472→	n, err := w.Write(buf)
   473→	return n, err
   474→}
   475→
   476→// readWALRecord reads one WAL record from the reader.
   477→// Returns (nil, nil) on clean EOF. Returns error on data corruption.
   478→// A CRC mismatch indicates a truncated trailing record (expected on crash).
   479→func readWALRecord(r io.Reader) (*WALMessage, error) {
   480→	var magic uint32
   481→	if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &magic); err != nil {
   482→		if err == io.EOF {
   483→			return nil, nil // Clean EOF
   484→		}
   485→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("read record magic: %w", err)
   486→	}
   487→
   488→	if magic != walRecordMagic {
   489→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid record magic 0x%08X (expected 0x%08X)", magic, walRecordMagic)
   490→	}
   491→
   492→	var payloadLen uint32
   493→	if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &payloadLen); err != nil {
   494→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("read payload length: %w", err)
   495→	}
   496→
   497→	if payloadLen > 1<<20 { // 1 MB sanity limit
   498→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("record payload too large: %d bytes", payloadLen)
   499→	}
   500→
   501→	payload := make([]byte, payloadLen)
   502→	if _, err := io.ReadFull(r, payload); err != nil {
   503→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("read payload: %w", err)
   504→	}
   505→
   506→	var storedCRC uint32
   507→	if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &storedCRC); err != nil {
   508→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("read CRC: %w", err)
   509→	}
   510→
   511→	if crc32.ChecksumIEEE(payload) != storedCRC {
   512→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("CRC mismatch (truncated write or corruption)")
   513→	}
   514→
   515→	return parseWALPayload(payload)
   516→}
   517→
   518→// parseWALPayload decodes a binary payload into a WALMessage.
   519→func parseWALPayload(payload []byte) (*WALMessage, error) {
   520→	if len(payload) < 8+2+1+4 {
   521→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("payload too short: %d bytes", len(payload))
   522→	}
   523→
   524→	offset := 0
   525→
   526→	// Timestamp (8 bytes)
   527→	ts := int64(binary.LittleEndian.Uint64(payload[offset : offset+8]))
   528→	offset += 8
   529→
   530→	// Metric name (2-byte length + bytes)
   531→	if offset+2 > len(payload) {
   532→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("metric name length overflows payload")
   533→	}
   534→	mLen := int(binary.LittleEndian.Uint16(payload[offset : offset+2]))
   535→	offset += 2
   536→
   537→	if offset+mLen > len(payload) {
   538→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("metric name overflows payload")
   539→	}
   540→	metricName := string(payload[offset : offset+mLen])
   541→	offset += mLen
   542→
   543→	// Selector count (1 byte)
   544→	if offset >= len(payload) {
   545→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("selector count overflows payload")
   546→	}
   547→	selCount := int(payload[offset])
   548→	offset++
   549→
   550→	selectors := make([]string, selCount)
   551→	for i := range selCount {
   552→		if offset >= len(payload) {
   553→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("selector[%d] length overflows payload", i)
   554→		}
   555→		sLen := int(payload[offset])
   556→		offset++
   557→
   558→		if offset+sLen > len(payload) {
   559→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("selector[%d] data overflows payload", i)
   560→		}
   561→		selectors[i] = string(payload[offset : offset+sLen])
   562→		offset += sLen
   563→	}
   564→
   565→	// Value (4 bytes, float32 bits)
   566→	if offset+4 > len(payload) {
   567→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("value overflows payload")
   568→	}
   569→	bits := binary.LittleEndian.Uint32(payload[offset : offset+4])
   570→	value := schema.Float(math.Float32frombits(bits))
   571→
   572→	return &WALMessage{
   573→		MetricName: metricName,
   574→		Timestamp:  ts,
   575→		Selector:   selectors,
   576→		Value:      value,
   577→	}, nil
   578→}
   579→
   580→// loadWALFile reads a WAL file and replays all valid records into the Level tree.
   581→// l is the host-level node. Corrupt or partial trailing records are silently skipped
   582→// (expected on crash). Records older than 'from' are skipped.
   583→func (l *Level) loadWALFile(m *MemoryStore, f *os.File, from int64) error {
   584→	br := bufio.NewReader(f)
   585→
   586→	// Verify file header magic.
   587→	var fileMagic uint32
   588→	if err := binary.Read(br, binary.LittleEndian, &fileMagic); err != nil {
   589→		if err == io.EOF {
   590→			return nil // Empty file, no data
   591→		}
   592→		return fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: read file header: %w", err)
   593→	}
   594→
   595→	if fileMagic != walFileMagic {
   596→		return fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: invalid file magic 0x%08X (expected 0x%08X)", fileMagic, walFileMagic)
   597→	}
   598→
   599→	// Cache level lookups to avoid repeated tree traversal.
   600→	lvlCache := make(map[string]*Level)
   601→
   602→	for {
   603→		msg, err := readWALRecord(br)
   604→		if err != nil {
   605→			// Truncated trailing record is expected after a crash; stop replaying.
   606→			cclog.Debugf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: stopping replay at corrupted/partial record: %v", err)
   607→			break
   608→		}
   609→		if msg == nil {
   610→			break // Clean EOF
   611→		}
   612→
   613→		if msg.Timestamp < from {
   614→			continue // Older than retention window
   615→		}
   616→
   617→		minfo, ok := m.Metrics[msg.MetricName]
   618→		if !ok {
   619→			continue // Unknown metric (config may have changed)
   620→		}
   621→
   622→		// Cache key is the null-separated selector path.
   623→		cacheKey := joinSelector(msg.Selector)
   624→		lvl, ok := lvlCache[cacheKey]
   625→		if !ok {
   626→			lvl = l.findLevelOrCreate(msg.Selector, len(m.Metrics))
   627→			lvlCache[cacheKey] = lvl
   628→		}
   629→
   630→		// Write directly to the buffer, same as WriteToLevel but without the
   631→		// global level lookup (we already have the right level).
   632→		lvl.lock.Lock()
   633→		b := lvl.metrics[minfo.offset]
   634→		if b == nil {
   635→			b = newBuffer(msg.Timestamp, minfo.Frequency)
   636→			lvl.metrics[minfo.offset] = b
   637→		}
   638→		nb, writeErr := b.write(msg.Timestamp, msg.Value)
   639→		if writeErr == nil && b != nb {
   640→			lvl.metrics[minfo.offset] = nb
   641→		}
   642→		// Ignore write errors for timestamps before buffer start (can happen when
   643→		// replaying WAL entries that predate a loaded snapshot's start time).
   644→		lvl.lock.Unlock()
   645→	}
   646→
   647→	return nil
   648→}
   649→
   650→// joinSelector builds a cache key from a selector slice using null bytes as separators.
   651→func joinSelector(sel []string) string {
   652→	if len(sel) == 0 {
   653→		return ""
   654→	}
   655→	var result strings.Builder
   656→	result.WriteString(sel[0])
   657→	for i := 1; i < len(sel); i++ {
   658→		result.WriteString("\x00" + sel[i])
   659→	}
   660→	return result.String()
   661→}
   662→
   663→// ToCheckpointWAL writes binary snapshot files for all hosts in parallel.
   664→// Returns the number of files written, the list of host directories that were
   665→// successfully checkpointed (for WAL rotation), and any errors.
   666→func (m *MemoryStore) ToCheckpointWAL(dir string, from, to int64) (int, []string, error) {
   667→	// Collect all cluster/host pairs.
   668→	m.root.lock.RLock()
   669→	totalHosts := 0
   670→	for _, l1 := range m.root.children {
   671→		l1.lock.RLock()
   672→		totalHosts += len(l1.children)
   673→		l1.lock.RUnlock()
   674→	}
   675→	m.root.lock.RUnlock()
   676→
   677→	levels := make([]*Level, 0, totalHosts)
   678→	selectors := make([][]string, 0, totalHosts)
   679→
   680→	m.root.lock.RLock()
   681→	for sel1, l1 := range m.root.children {
   682→		l1.lock.RLock()
   683→		for sel2, l2 := range l1.children {
   684→			levels = append(levels, l2)
   685→			selectors = append(selectors, []string{sel1, sel2})
   686→		}
   687→		l1.lock.RUnlock()
   688→	}
   689→	m.root.lock.RUnlock()
   690→
   691→	type workItem struct {
   692→		level    *Level
   693→		hostDir  string
   694→		selector []string
   695→	}
   696→
   697→	totalWork := len(levels)
   698→	cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> Starting binary checkpoint for %d hosts with %d workers", totalWork, Keys.NumWorkers)
   699→
   700→	n, errs, completed := int32(0), int32(0), int32(0)
   701→	var successDirs []string
   702→	var successMu sync.Mutex
   703→
   704→	var wg sync.WaitGroup
   705→	wg.Add(Keys.NumWorkers)
   706→	work := make(chan workItem, Keys.NumWorkers*2)
   707→
   708→	// Progress logging goroutine.
   709→	stopProgress := make(chan struct{})
   710→	go func() {
   711→		ticker := time.NewTicker(10 * time.Second)
   712→		defer ticker.Stop()
   713→		for {
   714→			select {
   715→			case <-ticker.C:
   716→				cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> Checkpoint progress: %d/%d hosts (%d written, %d errors)",
   717→					atomic.LoadInt32(&completed), totalWork, atomic.LoadInt32(&n), atomic.LoadInt32(&errs))
   718→			case <-stopProgress:
   719→				return
   720→			}
   721→		}
   722→	}()
   723→
   724→	for range Keys.NumWorkers {
   725→		go func() {
   726→			defer wg.Done()
   727→			for wi := range work {
   728→				err := wi.level.toCheckpointBinary(wi.hostDir, from, to, m)
   729→				if err != nil {
   730→					if err == ErrNoNewArchiveData {
   731→						atomic.AddInt32(&completed, 1)
   732→						continue
   733→					}
   734→					cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> binary checkpoint error for %s: %v", wi.hostDir, err)
   735→					atomic.AddInt32(&errs, 1)
   736→				} else {
   737→					atomic.AddInt32(&n, 1)
   738→					successMu.Lock()
   739→					successDirs = append(successDirs, wi.hostDir)
   740→					successMu.Unlock()
   741→				}
   742→				atomic.AddInt32(&completed, 1)
   743→			}
   744→		}()
   745→	}
   746→
   747→	for i := range levels {
   748→		hostDir := path.Join(dir, path.Join(selectors[i]...))
   749→		work <- workItem{
   750→			level:    levels[i],
   751→			hostDir:  hostDir,
   752→			selector: selectors[i],
   753→		}
   754→	}
   755→	close(work)
   756→	wg.Wait()
   757→	close(stopProgress)
   758→
   759→	if errs > 0 {
   760→		return int(n), successDirs, fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> %d errors during binary checkpoint (%d successes)", errs, n)
   761→	}
   762→	return int(n), successDirs, nil
   763→}
   764→
   765→// toCheckpointBinary writes a binary snapshot file for a single host-level node.
   766→// Uses atomic rename (write to .tmp then rename) to avoid partial reads on crash.
   767→func (l *Level) toCheckpointBinary(dir string, from, to int64, m *MemoryStore) error {
   768→	cf, err := l.toCheckpointFile(from, to, m)
   769→	if err != nil {
   770→		return err
   771→	}
   772→	if cf == nil {
   773→		return ErrNoNewArchiveData
   774→	}
   775→
   776→	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, CheckpointDirPerms); err != nil {
   777→		return fmt.Errorf("mkdir %s: %w", dir, err)
   778→	}
   779→
   780→	// Write to a temp file first, then rename (atomic on POSIX).
   781→	tmpPath := path.Join(dir, fmt.Sprintf("%d.bin.tmp", from))
   782→	finalPath := path.Join(dir, fmt.Sprintf("%d.bin", from))
   783→
   784→	f, err := os.OpenFile(tmpPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_TRUNC, CheckpointFilePerms)
   785→	if err != nil {
   786→		return fmt.Errorf("open binary snapshot %s: %w", tmpPath, err)
   787→	}
   788→
   789→	bw := bufio.NewWriter(f)
   790→	if err := writeBinarySnapshotFile(bw, cf); err != nil {
   791→		f.Close()
   792→		os.Remove(tmpPath)
   793→		return fmt.Errorf("write binary snapshot: %w", err)
   794→	}
   795→	if err := bw.Flush(); err != nil {
   796→		f.Close()
   797→		os.Remove(tmpPath)
   798→		return err
   799→	}
   800→	f.Close()
   801→
   802→	return os.Rename(tmpPath, finalPath)
   803→}
   804→
   805→// writeBinarySnapshotFile writes the binary snapshot file header and level tree.
   806→func writeBinarySnapshotFile(w io.Writer, cf *CheckpointFile) error {
   807→	if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, snapFileMagic); err != nil {
   808→		return err
   809→	}
   810→	if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, cf.From); err != nil {
   811→		return err
   812→	}
   813→	if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, cf.To); err != nil {
   814→		return err
   815→	}
   816→	return writeBinaryLevel(w, cf)
   817→}
   818→
   819→// writeBinaryLevel recursively writes a CheckpointFile level in binary format.
   820→func writeBinaryLevel(w io.Writer, cf *CheckpointFile) error {
   821→	if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, uint32(len(cf.Metrics))); err != nil {
   822→		return err
   823→	}
   824→
   825→	for name, metric := range cf.Metrics {
   826→		if err := writeString16(w, name); err != nil {
   827→			return err
   828→		}
   829→		if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, metric.Frequency); err != nil {
   830→			return err
   831→		}
   832→		if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, metric.Start); err != nil {
   833→			return err
   834→		}
   835→		if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, uint32(len(metric.Data))); err != nil {
   836→			return err
   837→		}
   838→		for _, v := range metric.Data {
   839→			if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, math.Float32bits(float32(v))); err != nil {
   840→				return err
   841→			}
   842→		}
   843→	}
   844→
   845→	if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, uint32(len(cf.Children))); err != nil {
   846→		return err
   847→	}
   848→
   849→	for name, child := range cf.Children {
   850→		if err := writeString16(w, name); err != nil {
   851→			return err
   852→		}
   853→		if err := writeBinaryLevel(w, child); err != nil {
   854→			return err
   855→		}
   856→	}
   857→
   858→	return nil
   859→}
   860→
   861→// writeString16 writes a 2-byte length-prefixed string to w.
   862→func writeString16(w io.Writer, s string) error {
   863→	if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, uint16(len(s))); err != nil {
   864→		return err
   865→	}
   866→	_, err := io.WriteString(w, s)
   867→	return err
   868→}
   869→
   870→// loadBinaryFile reads a binary snapshot file and loads data into the Level tree.
   871→// The retention check (from) is applied to the file's 'to' timestamp.
   872→func (l *Level) loadBinaryFile(m *MemoryStore, f *os.File, from int64) error {
   873→	br := bufio.NewReader(f)
   874→
   875→	var magic uint32
   876→	if err := binary.Read(br, binary.LittleEndian, &magic); err != nil {
   877→		return fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> binary snapshot: read magic: %w", err)
   878→	}
   879→	if magic != snapFileMagic {
   880→		return fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> binary snapshot: invalid magic 0x%08X (expected 0x%08X)", magic, snapFileMagic)
   881→	}
   882→
   883→	var fileFrom, fileTo int64
   884→	if err := binary.Read(br, binary.LittleEndian, &fileFrom); err != nil {
   885→		return fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> binary snapshot: read from: %w", err)
   886→	}
   887→	if err := binary.Read(br, binary.LittleEndian, &fileTo); err != nil {
   888→		return fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> binary snapshot: read to: %w", err)
   889→	}
   890→
   891→	if fileTo != 0 && fileTo < from {
   892→		return nil // File is older than retention window, skip it
   893→	}
   894→
   895→	cf, err := readBinaryLevel(br)
   896→	if err != nil {
   897→		return fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> binary snapshot: read level tree: %w", err)
   898→	}
   899→	cf.From = fileFrom
   900→	cf.To = fileTo
   901→
   902→	return l.loadFile(cf, m)
   903→}
   904→
   905→// readBinaryLevel recursively reads a level from the binary snapshot format.
   906→func readBinaryLevel(r io.Reader) (*CheckpointFile, error) {
   907→	cf := &CheckpointFile{
   908→		Metrics:  make(map[string]*CheckpointMetrics),
   909→		Children: make(map[string]*CheckpointFile),
   910→	}
   911→
   912→	var numMetrics uint32
   913→	if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &numMetrics); err != nil {
   914→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("read num_metrics: %w", err)
   915→	}
   916→
   917→	for range numMetrics {
   918→		name, err := readString16(r)
   919→		if err != nil {
   920→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read metric name: %w", err)
   921→		}
   922→
   923→		var freq, start int64
   924→		if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &freq); err != nil {
   925→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read frequency for %s: %w", name, err)
   926→		}
   927→		if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &start); err != nil {
   928→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read start for %s: %w", name, err)
   929→		}
   930→
   931→		var numValues uint32
   932→		if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &numValues); err != nil {
   933→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read num_values for %s: %w", name, err)
   934→		}
   935→
   936→		data := make([]schema.Float, numValues)
   937→		for i := range numValues {
   938→			var bits uint32
   939→			if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &bits); err != nil {
   940→				return nil, fmt.Errorf("read value[%d] for %s: %w", i, name, err)
   941→			}
   942→			data[i] = schema.Float(math.Float32frombits(bits))
   943→		}
   944→
   945→		cf.Metrics[name] = &CheckpointMetrics{
   946→			Frequency: freq,
   947→			Start:     start,
   948→			Data:      data,
   949→		}
   950→	}
   951→
   952→	var numChildren uint32
   953→	if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &numChildren); err != nil {
   954→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("read num_children: %w", err)
   955→	}
   956→
   957→	for range numChildren {
   958→		childName, err := readString16(r)
   959→		if err != nil {
   960→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read child name: %w", err)
   961→		}
   962→
   963→		child, err := readBinaryLevel(r)
   964→		if err != nil {
   965→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read child %s: %w", childName, err)
   966→		}
   967→		cf.Children[childName] = child
   968→	}
   969→
   970→	return cf, nil
   971→}
   972→
   973→// readString16 reads a 2-byte length-prefixed string from r.
   974→func readString16(r io.Reader) (string, error) {
   975→	var sLen uint16
   976→	if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &sLen); err != nil {
   977→		return "", err
   978→	}
   979→	buf := make([]byte, sLen)
   980→	if _, err := io.ReadFull(r, buf); err != nil {
   981→		return "", err
   982→	}
   983→	return string(buf), nil
   984→}

     5→
     6→// Package metricstore provides buffer.go: Time-series data buffer implementation.
     7→//
     8→// # Buffer Architecture
     9→//
    10→// Each metric at each hierarchical level (cluster/host/cpu/etc.) uses a linked-list
    11→// chain of fixed-size buffers to store time-series data. This design:
    12→//
    13→//   - Avoids reallocation/copying when growing (new links added instead)
    14→//   - Enables efficient pooling (buffers returned to sync.Pool)
    15→//   - Supports traversal back in time (via prev pointers)
    16→//   - Maintains temporal ordering (newer data in later buffers)
    17→//
    18→// # Buffer Chain Example
    19→//
    20→//	[oldest buffer] <- prev -- [older] <- prev -- [newest buffer (head)]
    21→//	  start=1000               start=1512           start=2024
    22→//	  data=[v0...v511]         data=[v0...v511]     data=[v0...v42]
    23→//
    24→// When the head buffer reaches capacity (BufferCap = 512), a new buffer becomes
    25→// the new head and the old head is linked via prev.
    26→//
    27→// # Pooling Strategy
    28→//
    29→// sync.Pool reduces GC pressure for the common case (BufferCap-sized allocations).
    30→// Non-standard capacity buffers are not pooled (e.g., from checkpoint deserialization).
    31→//
    32→// # Time Alignment
    33→//
    34→// Timestamps are aligned to measurement frequency intervals:
    35→//
    36→//	index = (timestamp - buffer.start) / buffer.frequency
    37→//	actualTime = buffer.start + (frequency / 2) + (index * frequency)
    38→//
    39→// Missing data points are represented as NaN values. The read() function performs
    40→// linear interpolation where possible.
    41→package metricstore
    42→
    43→import (
    44→	"errors"
    45→	"sync"
    46→	"time"
    47→
    48→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    49→)
    50→
    51→// BufferCap is the default buffer capacity.
    52→// buffer.data will only ever grow up to its capacity and a new link
    53→// in the buffer chain will be created if needed so that no copying
    54→// of data or reallocation needs to happen on writes.
    55→const BufferCap int = DefaultBufferCapacity
    56→
    57→// BufferPool is the global instance.
    58→// It is initialized immediately when the package loads.
    59→var bufferPool = NewPersistentBufferPool()
    60→
    61→type PersistentBufferPool struct {
    62→	pool []*buffer
    63→	mu   sync.Mutex
    64→}
    65→
    66→// NewPersistentBufferPool creates a dynamic pool for buffers.
    67→func NewPersistentBufferPool() *PersistentBufferPool {
    68→	return &PersistentBufferPool{
    69→		pool: make([]*buffer, 0),
    70→	}
    71→}
    72→
    73→func (p *PersistentBufferPool) Get() *buffer {
    74→	p.mu.Lock()
    75→	defer p.mu.Unlock()
    76→
    77→	n := len(p.pool)
    78→	if n == 0 {
    79→		// Pool is empty, allocate a new one
    80→		return &buffer{
    81→			data: make([]schema.Float, 0, BufferCap),
    82→		}
    83→	}
    84→
    85→	// Reuse existing buffer from the pool
    86→	b := p.pool[n-1]
    87→	p.pool[n-1] = nil // Avoid memory leak
    88→	p.pool = p.pool[:n-1]
    89→	return b
    90→}
    91→
    92→// Put returns b to the pool. The caller must set b.lastUsed = time.Now().Unix()
    93→// before calling Put so that Clean() can evict idle entries correctly.
    94→func (p *PersistentBufferPool) Put(b *buffer) {
    95→	// Reset the buffer before putting it back
    96→	b.data = b.data[:0]
    97→
    98→	p.mu.Lock()
    99→	defer p.mu.Unlock()
   100→
   101→	p.pool = append(p.pool, b)
   102→}
   103→
   104→// GetSize returns the exact number of buffers currently sitting in the pool.
   105→func (p *PersistentBufferPool) GetSize() int {
   106→	p.mu.Lock()
   107→	defer p.mu.Unlock()
   108→	return len(p.pool)
   109→}
   110→
   111→// Clear drains all buffers currently in the pool, allowing the GC to collect them.
   112→func (p *PersistentBufferPool) Clear() {
   113→	p.mu.Lock()
   114→	defer p.mu.Unlock()
   115→	for i := range p.pool {
   116→		p.pool[i] = nil
   117→	}
   118→	p.pool = p.pool[:0]
   119→}
   120→
   121→// Clean removes buffers from the pool that haven't been used in the given duration.
   122→// It uses a simple LRU approach based on the lastUsed timestamp.
   123→func (p *PersistentBufferPool) Clean(threshold int64) {
   124→	p.mu.Lock()
   125→	defer p.mu.Unlock()
   126→
   127→	// Filter in place, retaining only buffers returned to the pool recently enough.
   128→	active := p.pool[:0]
   129→	for _, b := range p.pool {
   130→		if b.lastUsed >= threshold {
   131→			active = append(active, b)
   132→		}
   133→	}
   134→
   135→	// Nullify the rest to prevent memory leaks
   136→	for i := len(active); i < len(p.pool); i++ {
   137→		p.pool[i] = nil
   138→	}
   139→
   140→	p.pool = active
   141→}
   142→
   143→var (
   144→	// ErrNoData indicates no time-series data exists for the requested metric/level.
   145→	ErrNoData error = errors.New("[METRICSTORE]> no data for this metric/level")
   146→
   147→	// ErrDataDoesNotAlign indicates that aggregated data from child scopes
   148→	// does not align with the parent scope's expected timestamps/intervals.
   149→	ErrDataDoesNotAlignMissingFront    error = errors.New("[METRICSTORE]> data from lower granularities does not align (missing data prior to start of the buffers)")
   150→	ErrDataDoesNotAlignMissingBack     error = errors.New("[METRICSTORE]> data from lower granularities does not align (missing data after the end of the buffers)")
   151→	ErrDataDoesNotAlignDataLenMismatch error = errors.New("[METRICSTORE]> data from lower granularities does not align (collected data length is different than expected data length)")
   152→)
   153→
   154→// buffer stores time-series data for a single metric at a specific hierarchical level.
   155→//
   156→// Buffers form doubly-linked chains ordered by time. When capacity is reached,
   157→// a new buffer becomes the head and the old head is linked via prev/next.
   158→//
   159→// Fields:
   160→//   - prev:      Link to older buffer in the chain (nil if this is oldest)
   161→//   - next:      Link to newer buffer in the chain (nil if this is newest/head)
   162→//   - data:      Time-series values (schema.Float supports NaN for missing data)
   163→//   - frequency: Measurement interval in seconds
   164→//   - start:     Start timestamp (adjusted by -frequency/2 for alignment)
   165→//   - archived:  True if data has been persisted to disk archive
   166→//   - closed:    True if buffer is no longer accepting writes
   167→//
   168→// Index calculation: index = (timestamp - start) / frequency
   169→// Actual data timestamp: start + (frequency / 2) + (index * frequency)
   170→type buffer struct {
   171→	prev      *buffer
   172→	next      *buffer
   173→	data      []schema.Float
   174→	frequency int64
   175→	start     int64
   176→	archived  bool
   177→	closed    bool
   178→	lastUsed  int64
   179→}
   180→
   181→func newBuffer(ts, freq int64) *buffer {
   182→	b := bufferPool.Get()
   183→	b.frequency = freq
   184→	b.start = ts - (freq / 2)
   185→	b.prev = nil
   186→	b.next = nil
   187→	b.archived = false
   188→	b.closed = false
   189→	b.data = b.data[:0]
   190→	return b
   191→}
   192→
   193→// write appends a timestamped value to the buffer chain.
   194→//
   195→// Returns the head buffer (which may be newly created if capacity was reached).
   196→// Timestamps older than the buffer's start are rejected. If the calculated index
   197→// exceeds capacity, a new buffer is allocated and linked as the new head.
   198→//
   199→// Missing timestamps are automatically filled with NaN values to maintain alignment.
   200→// Overwrites are allowed if the index is already within the existing data slice.
   201→//
   202→// Parameters:
   203→//   - ts:    Unix timestamp in seconds
   204→//   - value: Metric value (can be schema.NaN for missing data)
   205→//
   206→// Returns:
   207→//   - *buffer: The new head buffer (same as b if no new buffer created)
   208→//   - error:   Non-nil if timestamp is before buffer start
   209→func (b *buffer) write(ts int64, value schema.Float) (*buffer, error) {
   210→	if ts < b.start {
   211→		return nil, errors.New("[METRICSTORE]> cannot write value to buffer from past")
   212→	}
   213→
   214→	// idx := int((ts - b.start + (b.frequency / 3)) / b.frequency)
   215→	idx := int((ts - b.start) / b.frequency)
   216→	if idx >= cap(b.data) {
   217→		newbuf := newBuffer(ts, b.frequency)
   218→		newbuf.prev = b
   219→		b.next = newbuf
   220→		b = newbuf
   221→		idx = 0
   222→	}
   223→
   224→	// Overwriting value or writing value from past
   225→	if idx < len(b.data) {
   226→		b.data[idx] = value
   227→		return b, nil
   228→	}
   229→
   230→	// Fill up unwritten slots with NaN
   231→	for i := len(b.data); i < idx; i++ {
   232→		b.data = append(b.data, schema.NaN)
   233→	}
   234→
   235→	b.data = append(b.data, value)
   236→	return b, nil
   237→}
   238→
   239→func (b *buffer) end() int64 {
   240→	return b.firstWrite() + int64(len(b.data))*b.frequency
   241→}
   242→
   243→func (b *buffer) firstWrite() int64 {
   244→	return b.start + (b.frequency / 2)
   245→}
   246→
   247→// read retrieves time-series data from the buffer chain for the specified time range.
   248→//
   249→// Traverses the buffer chain backwards (via prev links) if 'from' precedes the current
   250→// buffer's start. Missing data points are represented as NaN. Values are accumulated
   251→// into the provided 'data' slice (using +=, so caller must zero-initialize if needed).
   252→//
   253→// The function adjusts the actual time range returned if data is unavailable at the
   254→// boundaries (returned via adjusted from/to timestamps).
   255→//
   256→// Parameters:
   257→//   - from: Start timestamp (Unix seconds)
   258→//   - to:   End timestamp (Unix seconds, exclusive)
   259→//   - data: Pre-allocated slice to accumulate results (must be large enough)
   260→//
   261→// Returns:
   262→//   - []schema.Float: Slice of data (may be shorter than input 'data' slice)
   263→//   - int64:          Actual start timestamp with available data
   264→//   - int64:          Actual end timestamp (exclusive)
   265→//   - error:          Non-nil on failure
   266→//
   267→// Panics if 'data' slice is too small to hold all values in [from, to).
   268→func (b *buffer) read(from, to int64, data []schema.Float) ([]schema.Float, int64, int64, error) {
   269→	// Walk back to the buffer that covers 'from', adjusting if we hit the oldest.
   270→	for from < b.firstWrite() {
   271→		if b.prev == nil {
   272→			from = b.firstWrite()
   273→			break
   274→		}
   275→		b = b.prev
   276→	}
   277→
   278→	i := 0
   279→	t := from
   280→	for ; t < to; t += b.frequency {
   281→		idx := int((t - b.start) / b.frequency)
   282→		if idx >= cap(b.data) {
   283→			if b.next == nil {
   284→				break
   285→			}
   286→			b = b.next
   287→			// Recalculate idx in the new buffer; a gap between buffers may exist.
   288→			idx = int((t - b.start) / b.frequency)
   289→		}
   290→
   291→		if idx >= len(b.data) {
   292→			if b.next == nil || to <= b.next.start {
   293→				break
   294→			}
   295→			data[i] += schema.NaN // NaN + anything = NaN; propagates missing data
   296→		} else if t < b.start {
   297→			data[i] += schema.NaN // gap before this buffer's first write
   298→		} else {
   299→			data[i] += b.data[idx]
   300→		}
   301→		i++
   302→	}
   303→
   304→	return data[:i], from, t, nil
   305→}
   306→
   307→// free removes buffers older than the specified timestamp from the chain.
   308→//
   309→// Recursively traverses backwards (via prev) and unlinks buffers whose end time
   310→// is before the retention threshold. Freed buffers are returned to the pool if
   311→// they have the standard capacity (BufferCap).
   312→//
   313→// Parameters:
   314→//   - t: Retention threshold timestamp (Unix seconds)
   315→//
   316→// Returns:
   317→//   - delme: True if the current buffer itself should be deleted by caller
   318→//   - n:     Number of buffers freed in this subtree
   319→func (b *buffer) free(t int64) (delme bool, n int) {
   320→	if b.prev != nil {
   321→		delme, m := b.prev.free(t)
   322→		n += m
   323→		if delme {
   324→			b.prev.next = nil
   325→			if cap(b.prev.data) != BufferCap {
   326→				b.prev.data = make([]schema.Float, 0, BufferCap)
   327→			}
   328→			b.prev.lastUsed = time.Now().Unix()
   329→			bufferPool.Put(b.prev)
   330→			b.prev = nil
   331→		}
   332→	}
   333→
   334→	end := b.end()
   335→	if end < t {
   336→		return true, n + 1
   337→	}
   338→
   339→	return false, n
   340→}
   341→
   342→// forceFreeOldest recursively finds the end of the linked list (the oldest buffer)
   343→// and removes it.
   344→// Returns:
   345→//
   346→//	delme: true if 'b' itself is the oldest and should be removed by the caller
   347→//	n:     the number of buffers freed (will be 1 or 0)
   348→func (b *buffer) forceFreeOldest() (delme bool, n int) {
   349→	// If there is a previous buffer, recurse down to find the oldest
   350→	if b.prev != nil {
   351→		delPrev, freed := b.prev.forceFreeOldest()
   352→
   353→		// If the previous buffer signals it should be deleted:
   354→		if delPrev {
   355→			b.prev.next = nil
   356→			if cap(b.prev.data) != BufferCap {
   357→				b.prev.data = make([]schema.Float, 0, BufferCap)
   358→			}
   359→			b.prev.lastUsed = time.Now().Unix()
   360→			bufferPool.Put(b.prev)
   361→			b.prev = nil
   362→		}
   363→		return false, freed
   364→	}
   365→
   366→	// If b.prev is nil, THIS buffer is the oldest.
   367→	// We return true so the parent (or the Level loop) knows to delete reference to 'b'.
   368→	return true, 1
   369→}
   370→
   371→// iterFromTo invokes callback on every buffer in the chain that overlaps [from, to].
   372→//
   373→// Traverses backwards (via prev) first, then processes current buffer if it overlaps
   374→// the time range. Used for checkpoint/archive operations that need to serialize buffers
   375→// within a specific time window.
   376→//
   377→// Parameters:
   378→//   - from:     Start timestamp (Unix seconds, inclusive)
   379→//   - to:       End timestamp (Unix seconds, inclusive)
   380→//   - callback: Function to invoke on each overlapping buffer
   381→//
   382→// Returns:
   383→//   - error: First error returned by callback, or nil if all succeeded
   384→func (b *buffer) iterFromTo(from, to int64, callback func(b *buffer) error) error {
   385→	if b == nil {
   386→		return nil
   387→	}
   388→
   389→	// Collect overlapping buffers walking backwards (newest → oldest).
   390→	var matching []*buffer
   391→	for cur := b; cur != nil; cur = cur.prev {
   392→		if from <= cur.end() && cur.start <= to {
   393→			matching = append(matching, cur)
   394→		}
   395→	}
   396→
   397→	// Invoke callback in chronological order (oldest → newest).
   398→	for i := len(matching) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
   399→		if err := callback(matching[i]); err != nil {
   400→			return err
   401→		}
   402→	}
   403→	return nil
   404→}
   405→
   406→func (b *buffer) count() int64 {
   407→	var res int64
   408→	for ; b != nil; b = b.prev {
   409→		res += int64(len(b.data))
   410→	}
   411→	return res
   412→}
   413→
   414→// bufferCount returns the number of buffer nodes in the linked list.
   415→func (b *buffer) bufferCount() int64 {
   416→	var n int64
   417→	for ; b != nil; b = b.prev {
   418→		n++
   419→	}
   420→	return n
   421→}

     6→// Package metricstore provides config.go: Configuration structures and metric management.
     7→//
     8→// # Configuration Hierarchy
     9→//
    10→// The metricstore package uses nested configuration structures:
    11→//
    12→//	MetricStoreConfig (Keys)
    13→//	├─ NumWorkers: Parallel checkpoint/archive workers
    14→//	├─ RetentionInMemory: How long to keep data in RAM (also used as cleanup interval)
    15→//	├─ MemoryCap: Memory limit in bytes (triggers forceFree)
    16→//	├─ Checkpoints: Persistence configuration
    17→//	│  ├─ FileFormat: "json" or "wal" (default: "wal")
    18→//	│  └─ RootDir: Checkpoint storage path
    19→//	├─ Cleanup: Long-term storage configuration (interval = RetentionInMemory)
    20→//	│  ├─ RootDir: Archive storage path (archive mode only)
    21→//	│  └─ Mode: "delete" or "archive"
    22→//	├─ Debug: Development/debugging options
    23→//	└─ Subscriptions: NATS topic subscriptions for metric ingestion
    24→//
    25→// # Metric Configuration
    26→//
    27→// Each metric (e.g., "cpu_load", "mem_used") has a MetricConfig entry in the global
    28→// Metrics map, defining:
    29→//
    30→//   - Frequency: Measurement interval in seconds
    31→//   - Aggregation: How to combine values (sum/avg/none) when transforming scopes
    32→//   - offset: Internal index into Level.metrics slice (assigned during Init)
    33→//
    34→// # AggregationStrategy
    35→//
    36→// Determines how to combine metric values when aggregating from finer to coarser scopes:
    37→//
    38→//   - NoAggregation: Do not combine (incompatible scopes)
    39→//   - SumAggregation: Add values (e.g., power consumption: core→socket)
    40→//   - AvgAggregation: Average values (e.g., temperature: core→socket)
    41→package metricstore
    42→
    43→import (
    44→	"fmt"
    45→	"time"
    46→
    47→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/pkg/archive"
    48→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    49→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    50→)
    51→
    52→const (
    53→	DefaultMaxWorkers                 = 10
    54→	DefaultBufferCapacity             = 512
    55→	DefaultGCTriggerInterval          = 100
    56→	DefaultMemoryUsageTrackerInterval = 5 * time.Minute
    57→)
    58→
    59→// Checkpoints configures periodic persistence of in-memory metric data.
    60→//
    61→// Fields:
    62→//   - FileFormat:  "json" (human-readable, periodic) or "wal" (binary snapshot + WAL, crash-safe); default is "wal"
    63→//   - RootDir:     Filesystem path for checkpoint files (created if missing)
    64→//   - MaxWALSize:  Maximum size in bytes for a single host's WAL file; 0 = unlimited (default).
    65→//     When exceeded the WAL is force-rotated to prevent unbounded disk growth.
    66→type Checkpoints struct {
    67→	FileFormat string `json:"file-format"`
    68→	RootDir    string `json:"directory"`
    69→	MaxWALSize int64  `json:"max-wal-size,omitempty"`
    70→}
    71→
    72→// Debug provides development and profiling options.
    73→//
    74→// Fields:
    75→//   - DumpToFile: Path to dump checkpoint data for inspection (empty = disabled)
    76→//   - EnableGops: Enable gops agent for live runtime debugging (https://github.com/google/gops)
    77→type Debug struct {
    78→	DumpToFile string `json:"dump-to-file"`
    79→	EnableGops bool   `json:"gops"`
    80→}
    81→
    82→// Cleanup configures long-term storage of old metric data.
    83→//
    84→// Data older than RetentionInMemory is archived to disk or deleted.
    85→// The cleanup interval is always RetentionInMemory.
    86→//
    87→// Fields:
    88→//   - RootDir: Filesystem path for archived data (used in "archive" mode)
    89→//   - Mode:    "delete" (discard old data) or "archive" (write to RootDir)
    90→type Cleanup struct {
    91→	RootDir string `json:"directory"`
    92→	Mode    string `json:"mode"`
    93→}
    94→
    95→// Subscriptions defines NATS topics to subscribe to for metric ingestion.
    96→//
    97→// Each subscription receives metrics via NATS messaging, enabling real-time
    98→// data collection from compute nodes.
    99→//
   100→// Fields:
   101→//   - SubscribeTo: NATS subject/channel name (e.g., "metrics.compute.*")
   102→//   - ClusterTag:  Default cluster name for metrics without cluster tag (optional)
   103→type Subscriptions []struct {
   104→	// Channel name
   105→	SubscribeTo string `json:"subscribe-to"`
   106→
   107→	// Allow lines without a cluster tag, use this as default, optional
   108→	ClusterTag string `json:"cluster-tag"`
   109→}
   110→
   111→// MetricStoreConfig defines the main configuration for the metricstore.
   112→//
   113→// Loaded from cc-backend's config.json "metricstore" section. Controls memory usage,
   114→// persistence, archiving, and metric ingestion.
   115→//
   116→// Fields:
   117→//   - NumWorkers:        Parallel workers for checkpoint/archive (0 = auto: min(NumCPU/2+1, 10))
   118→//   - RetentionInMemory: Duration string (e.g., "48h") for in-memory data retention
   119→//   - MemoryCap:         Max memory in GB for buffer data (0 = unlimited); triggers forceFree when exceeded
   120→//   - Checkpoints:       Periodic persistence configuration
   121→//   - Debug:             Development/profiling options (nil = disabled)
   122→//   - Archive:           Long-term storage configuration (nil = disabled)
   123→//   - Subscriptions:     NATS topics for metric ingestion (nil = polling only)
   124→type MetricStoreConfig struct {
   125→	// Number of concurrent workers for checkpoint and archive operations.
   126→	// If not set or 0, defaults to min(runtime.NumCPU()/2+1, 10)
   127→	NumWorkers         int            `json:"num-workers"`
   128→	RetentionInMemory  string         `json:"retention-in-memory"`
   129→	CheckpointInterval string         `json:"checkpoint-interval,omitempty"`
   130→	MemoryCap          int            `json:"memory-cap"`
   131→	Checkpoints        Checkpoints    `json:"checkpoints"`
   132→	Debug              *Debug         `json:"debug"`
   133→	Cleanup            *Cleanup       `json:"cleanup"`
   134→	Subscriptions      *Subscriptions `json:"nats-subscriptions"`
   135→}
   136→
   137→// Keys is the global metricstore configuration instance.
   138→//
   139→// Initialized with defaults, then overwritten by cc-backend's config.json.
   140→// Accessed by Init(), Checkpointing(), and other lifecycle functions.
   141→var Keys MetricStoreConfig = MetricStoreConfig{
   142→	Checkpoints: Checkpoints{
   143→		FileFormat: "wal",
   144→		RootDir:    "./var/checkpoints",
   145→	},
   146→	Cleanup: &Cleanup{
   147→		Mode: "delete",
   148→	},
   149→}
   150→
   151→// AggregationStrategy defines how to combine metric values across hierarchy levels.
   152→//
   153→// Used when transforming data from finer-grained scopes (e.g., core) to coarser scopes
   154→// (e.g., socket). This is SPATIAL aggregation, not TEMPORAL (time-based) aggregation.
   155→//
   156→// Values:
   157→//   - NoAggregation:  Do not aggregate (incompatible scopes or non-aggregatable metrics)
   158→//   - SumAggregation: Add values (e.g., power: sum core power → socket power)
   159→//   - AvgAggregation: Average values (e.g., temperature: average core temps → socket temp)
   160→type AggregationStrategy int
   161→
   162→const (
   163→	NoAggregation  AggregationStrategy = iota // Do not aggregate
   164→	SumAggregation                            // Sum values (e.g., power, energy)
   165→	AvgAggregation                            // Average values (e.g., temperature, utilization)
   166→)
   167→
   168→// AssignAggregationStrategy parses a string into an AggregationStrategy value.
   169→//
   170→// Used when loading metric configurations from JSON/YAML files.
   171→//
   172→// Parameters:
   173→//   - str: "sum", "avg", or "" (empty string for NoAggregation)
   174→//
   175→// Returns:
   176→//   - AggregationStrategy: Parsed value
   177→//   - error:               Non-nil if str is unrecognized
   178→func AssignAggregationStrategy(str string) (AggregationStrategy, error) {
   179→	switch str {
   180→	case "":
   181→		return NoAggregation, nil
   182→	case "sum":
   183→		return SumAggregation, nil
   184→	case "avg":
   185→		return AvgAggregation, nil
   186→	default:
   187→		return NoAggregation, fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> unknown aggregation strategy: %s", str)
   188→	}
   189→}
   190→
   191→// MetricConfig defines configuration for a single metric type.
   192→//
   193→// Stored in the global Metrics map, keyed by metric name (e.g., "cpu_load").
   194→//
   195→// Fields:
   196→//   - Frequency:   Measurement interval in seconds (e.g., 60 for 1-minute granularity)
   197→//   - Aggregation: How to combine values across hierarchy levels (sum/avg/none)
   198→//   - offset:      Internal index into Level.metrics slice (assigned during Init)
   199→type MetricConfig struct {
   200→	// Interval in seconds at which measurements are stored
   201→	Frequency int64
   202→
   203→	// Can be 'sum', 'avg' or null. Describes how to aggregate metrics from the same timestep over the hierarchy.
   204→	Aggregation AggregationStrategy
   205→
   206→	// Private, used internally...
   207→	offset int
   208→}
   209→
   210→func BuildMetricList() map[string]MetricConfig {
   211→	var metrics map[string]MetricConfig = make(map[string]MetricConfig)
   212→
   213→	addMetric := func(name string, metric MetricConfig) error {
   214→		if metrics == nil {
   215→			metrics = make(map[string]MetricConfig, 0)
   216→		}
   217→
   218→		if existingMetric, ok := metrics[name]; ok {
   219→			if existingMetric.Frequency != metric.Frequency {
   220→				if existingMetric.Frequency < metric.Frequency {
   221→					existingMetric.Frequency = metric.Frequency
   222→					metrics[name] = existingMetric
   223→				}
   224→			}
   225→		} else {
   226→			metrics[name] = metric
   227→		}
   228→
   229→		return nil
   230→	}
   231→
   232→	// Helper function to add metric configuration
   233→	addMetricConfig := func(mc *schema.MetricConfig) {
   234→		agg, err := AssignAggregationStrategy(mc.Aggregation)
   235→		if err != nil {
   236→			cclog.Warnf("Could not find aggregation strategy for metric config '%s': %s", mc.Name, err.Error())
   237→		}
   238→
   239→		addMetric(mc.Name, MetricConfig{
   240→			Frequency:   int64(mc.Timestep),
   241→			Aggregation: agg,
   242→		})
   243→	}
   244→	for _, c := range archive.Clusters {
   245→		for _, mc := range c.MetricConfig {
   246→			addMetricConfig(mc)
   247→		}
   248→
   249→		for _, sc := range c.SubClusters {
   250→			for _, mc := range sc.MetricConfig {
   251→				addMetricConfig(mc)
   252→			}
   253→		}
   254→	}
   255→
   256→	return metrics
   257→}

     6→package metricstore
     7→
     8→import (
     9→	"encoding/json"
    10→	"fmt"
    11→	"time"
    12→
    13→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    14→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    15→)
    16→
    17→// HealthCheckResponse represents the result of a health check operation.
    18→type HealthCheckResponse struct {
    19→	Status schema.MonitoringState
    20→	Error  error
    21→}
    22→
    23→// HealthCheckResult holds the monitoring state and raw JSON health metrics
    24→// for a single node as determined by HealthCheck.
    25→type HealthCheckResult struct {
    26→	State         schema.MonitoringState
    27→	HealthMetrics string // JSON: {"missing":[...],"degraded":[...]}
    28→}
    29→
    30→// MaxMissingDataPoints is the threshold for stale data detection.
    31→// A buffer is considered healthy if the gap between its last data point
    32→// and the current time is within MaxMissingDataPoints * frequency.
    33→const MaxMissingDataPoints int64 = 5
    34→
    35→// bufferExists returns true if the buffer is non-nil and contains data.
    36→func (b *buffer) bufferExists() bool {
    37→	if b == nil || b.data == nil || len(b.data) == 0 {
    38→		return false
    39→	}
    40→
    41→	return true
    42→}
    43→
    44→// isBufferHealthy returns true if the buffer has recent data within
    45→// MaxMissingDataPoints * frequency of the current time.
    46→func (b *buffer) isBufferHealthy() bool {
    47→	bufferEnd := b.start + b.frequency*int64(len(b.data))
    48→	t := time.Now().Unix()
    49→
    50→	return t-bufferEnd <= MaxMissingDataPoints*b.frequency
    51→}
    52→
    53→// collectMetricStatus walks the subtree rooted at l and classifies each
    54→// expected metric into the healthy or degraded map.
    55→//
    56→// Classification rules (evaluated per buffer, pessimistic):
    57→//   - A single stale buffer marks the metric as degraded permanently.
    58→//   - A healthy buffer only counts if no stale buffer has been seen.
    59→//   - Metrics absent from the global config or without any buffer remain
    60→//     in neither map and are later reported as missing.
    61→func (l *Level) collectMetricStatus(m *MemoryStore, expectedMetrics []string, healthy, degraded map[string]bool) {
    62→	l.lock.RLock()
    63→	defer l.lock.RUnlock()
    64→
    65→	for _, metricName := range expectedMetrics {
    66→		if degraded[metricName] {
    67→			continue // already degraded, cannot improve
    68→		}
    69→		mc, ok := m.Metrics[metricName]
    70→		if !ok {
    71→			continue // unknown metric, will be reported as missing
    72→		}
    73→		b := l.metrics[mc.offset]
    74→		if b.bufferExists() {
    75→			if !b.isBufferHealthy() {
    76→				degraded[metricName] = true
    77→				delete(healthy, metricName)
    78→			} else if !degraded[metricName] {
    79→				healthy[metricName] = true
    80→			}
    81→		}
    82→	}
    83→
    84→	for _, lvl := range l.children {
    85→		lvl.collectMetricStatus(m, expectedMetrics, healthy, degraded)
    86→	}
    87→}
    88→
    89→// getHealthyMetrics walks the complete subtree rooted at l and classifies
    90→// each expected metric by comparing the collected status against the
    91→// expected list.
    92→//
    93→// Returns:
    94→//   - missingList: metrics not found in global config or without any buffer
    95→//   - degradedList: metrics with at least one stale buffer in the subtree
    96→func (l *Level) getHealthyMetrics(m *MemoryStore, expectedMetrics []string) ([]string, []string) {
    97→	healthy := make(map[string]bool, len(expectedMetrics))
    98→	degraded := make(map[string]bool)
    99→
   100→	l.collectMetricStatus(m, expectedMetrics, healthy, degraded)
   101→
   102→	missingList := make([]string, 0)
   103→	degradedList := make([]string, 0)
   104→
   105→	for _, metricName := range expectedMetrics {
   106→		if healthy[metricName] {
   107→			continue
   108→		}
   109→
   110→		if degraded[metricName] {
   111→			degradedList = append(degradedList, metricName)
   112→		} else {
   113→			missingList = append(missingList, metricName)
   114→		}
   115→	}
   116→
   117→	return degradedList, missingList
   118→}
   119→
   120→// GetHealthyMetrics returns missing and degraded metric lists for a node.
   121→//
   122→// It walks the metric tree starting from the node identified by selector
   123→// and classifies each expected metric:
   124→//   - Missing: no buffer anywhere in the subtree, or metric not in global config
   125→//   - Degraded: at least one stale buffer exists in the subtree
   126→//
   127→// Metrics present in expectedMetrics but absent from both returned lists
   128→// are considered fully healthy.
   129→func (m *MemoryStore) GetHealthyMetrics(selector []string, expectedMetrics []string) ([]string, []string, error) {
   130→	lvl := m.root.findLevel(selector)
   131→	if lvl == nil {
   132→		return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> GetHealthyMetrics: host not found: %#v", selector)
   133→	}
   134→
   135→	degradedList, missingList := lvl.getHealthyMetrics(m, expectedMetrics)
   136→	return degradedList, missingList, nil
   137→}
   138→
   139→type HealthCheckReq struct {
   140→	Cluster     string   `json:"cluster" example:"fritz"`
   141→	Nodes       []string `json:"nodes"`
   142→	MetricNames []string `json:"metric-names"`
   143→}
   144→
   145→// HealthCheck evaluates multiple nodes against a set of expected metrics
   146→// and returns a monitoring state per node.
   147→//
   148→// States:
   149→//   - MonitoringStateFull: all expected metrics are healthy
   150→//   - MonitoringStatePartial: some metrics are missing or degraded
   151→//   - MonitoringStateFailed: node not found, or no healthy metrics at all
   152→func (m *MemoryStore) HealthCheck(cluster string,
   153→	nodes []string, expectedMetrics []string,
   154→) (map[string]HealthCheckResult, error) {
   155→	results := make(map[string]HealthCheckResult, len(nodes))
   156→
   157→	for _, hostname := range nodes {
   158→		selector := []string{cluster, hostname}
   159→
   160→		degradedList, missingList, err := m.GetHealthyMetrics(selector, expectedMetrics)
   161→		if err != nil {
   162→			results[hostname] = HealthCheckResult{State: schema.MonitoringStateFailed}
   163→			continue
   164→		}
   165→
   166→		degradedCount := len(degradedList)
   167→		missingCount := len(missingList)
   168→
   169→		healthyCount := len(expectedMetrics) - degradedCount - missingCount
   170→
   171→		if degradedCount > 0 {
   172→			cclog.ComponentDebug("metricstore", "HealthCheck: node ", hostname, "degraded metrics:", degradedList)
   173→		}
   174→		if missingCount > 0 {
   175→			cclog.ComponentDebug("metricstore", "HealthCheck: node ", hostname, "missing metrics:", missingList)
   176→		}
   177→
   178→		var state schema.MonitoringState
   179→		switch {
   180→		case degradedCount == 0 && missingCount == 0:
   181→			state = schema.MonitoringStateFull
   182→		case healthyCount == 0:
   183→			state = schema.MonitoringStateFailed
   184→		default:
   185→			state = schema.MonitoringStatePartial
   186→		}
   187→
   188→		hm, _ := json.Marshal(map[string][]string{
   189→			"missing":  missingList,
   190→			"degraded": degradedList,
   191→		})
   192→
   193→		results[hostname] = HealthCheckResult{
   194→			State:         state,
   195→			HealthMetrics: string(hm),
   196→		}
   197→	}
   198→
   199→	return results, nil
   200→}
