     6→// This file contains shared scope transformation logic used by both the internal
     7→// metric store (pkg/metricstore) and the external cc-metric-store client
     8→// (internal/metricstoreclient). It extracts the common algorithm for mapping
     9→// between native metric scopes and requested scopes based on cluster topology.
    10→package metricstore
    11→
    12→import (
    13→	"strconv"
    14→
    15→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    16→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    17→)
    18→
    19→// Pre-converted scope strings avoid repeated string(MetricScope) allocations
    20→// during query construction. Used in ScopeQueryResult.Type field.
    21→var (
    22→	HWThreadString     = string(schema.MetricScopeHWThread)
    23→	CoreString         = string(schema.MetricScopeCore)
    24→	MemoryDomainString = string(schema.MetricScopeMemoryDomain)
    25→	SocketString       = string(schema.MetricScopeSocket)
    26→	AcceleratorString  = string(schema.MetricScopeAccelerator)
    27→)
    28→
    29→// ScopeQueryResult is a package-independent intermediate type returned by
    30→// BuildScopeQueries. Each consumer converts it to their own APIQuery type
    31→// (adding Resolution and any other package-specific fields).
    32→type ScopeQueryResult struct {
    33→	Type      *string
    34→	Metric    string
    35→	Hostname  string
    36→	TypeIds   []string
    37→	Scope     schema.MetricScope
    38→	Aggregate bool
    39→}
    40→
    41→// BuildScopeQueries generates scope query results for a given scope transformation.
    42→// It returns a slice of results and a boolean indicating success.
    43→// An empty slice means an expected exception (skip this combination).
    44→// ok=false means an unhandled case (caller should return an error).
    45→func BuildScopeQueries(
    46→	nativeScope, requestedScope schema.MetricScope,
    47→	metric, hostname string,
    48→	topology *schema.Topology,
    49→	hwthreads []int,
    50→	accelerators []string,
    51→) ([]ScopeQueryResult, bool) {
    52→	scope := nativeScope.Max(requestedScope)
    53→	results := []ScopeQueryResult{}
    54→
    55→	hwthreadsStr := IntToStringSlice(hwthreads)
    56→
    57→	// Accelerator -> Accelerator (Use "accelerator" scope if requested scope is lower than node)
    58→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeAccelerator && scope.LT(schema.MetricScopeNode) {
    59→		if scope != schema.MetricScopeAccelerator {
    60→			// Expected Exception -> Return Empty Slice
    61→			return results, true
    62→		}
    63→
    64→		results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
    65→			Metric:    metric,
    66→			Hostname:  hostname,
    67→			Aggregate: false,
    68→			Type:      &AcceleratorString,
    69→			TypeIds:   accelerators,
    70→			Scope:     schema.MetricScopeAccelerator,
    71→		})
    72→		return results, true
    73→	}
    74→
    75→	// Accelerator -> Node
    76→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeAccelerator && scope == schema.MetricScopeNode {
    77→		if len(accelerators) == 0 {
    78→			// Expected Exception -> Return Empty Slice
    79→			return results, true
    80→		}
    81→
    82→		results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
    83→			Metric:    metric,
    84→			Hostname:  hostname,
    85→			Aggregate: true,
    86→			Type:      &AcceleratorString,
    87→			TypeIds:   accelerators,
    88→			Scope:     scope,
    89→		})
    90→		return results, true
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	// HWThread -> HWThread
    94→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeHWThread && scope == schema.MetricScopeHWThread {
    95→		results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
    96→			Metric:    metric,
    97→			Hostname:  hostname,
    98→			Aggregate: false,
    99→			Type:      &HWThreadString,
   100→			TypeIds:   hwthreadsStr,
   101→			Scope:     scope,
   102→		})
   103→		return results, true
   104→	}
   105→
   106→	// HWThread -> Core
   107→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeHWThread && scope == schema.MetricScopeCore {
   108→		cores, _ := topology.GetCoresFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   109→		for _, core := range cores {
   110→			results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
   111→				Metric:    metric,
   112→				Hostname:  hostname,
   113→				Aggregate: true,
   114→				Type:      &HWThreadString,
   115→				TypeIds:   IntToStringSlice(topology.Core[core]),
   116→				Scope:     scope,
   117→			})
   118→		}
   119→		return results, true
   120→	}
   121→
   122→	// HWThread -> Socket
   123→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeHWThread && scope == schema.MetricScopeSocket {
   124→		sockets, _ := topology.GetSocketsFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   125→		for _, socket := range sockets {
   126→			results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
   127→				Metric:    metric,
   128→				Hostname:  hostname,
   129→				Aggregate: true,
   130→				Type:      &HWThreadString,
   131→				TypeIds:   IntToStringSlice(topology.Socket[socket]),
   132→				Scope:     scope,
   133→			})
   134→		}
   135→		return results, true
   136→	}
   137→
   138→	// HWThread -> Node
   139→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeHWThread && scope == schema.MetricScopeNode {
   140→		results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
   141→			Metric:    metric,
   142→			Hostname:  hostname,
   143→			Aggregate: true,
   144→			Type:      &HWThreadString,
   145→			TypeIds:   hwthreadsStr,
   146→			Scope:     scope,
   147→		})
   148→		return results, true
   149→	}
   150→
   151→	// Core -> Core
   152→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeCore && scope == schema.MetricScopeCore {
   153→		cores, _ := topology.GetCoresFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   154→		results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
   155→			Metric:    metric,
   156→			Hostname:  hostname,
   157→			Aggregate: false,
   158→			Type:      &CoreString,
   159→			TypeIds:   IntToStringSlice(cores),
   160→			Scope:     scope,
   161→		})
   162→		return results, true
   163→	}
   164→
   165→	// Core -> Socket
   166→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeCore && scope == schema.MetricScopeSocket {
   167→		sockets, _ := topology.GetSocketsFromCores(hwthreads)
   168→		for _, socket := range sockets {
   169→			results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
   170→				Metric:    metric,
   171→				Hostname:  hostname,
   172→				Aggregate: true,
   173→				Type:      &CoreString,
   174→				TypeIds:   IntToStringSlice(topology.Socket[socket]),
   175→				Scope:     scope,
   176→			})
   177→		}
   178→		return results, true
   179→	}
   180→
   181→	// Core -> Node
   182→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeCore && scope == schema.MetricScopeNode {
   183→		cores, _ := topology.GetCoresFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   184→		results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
   185→			Metric:    metric,
   186→			Hostname:  hostname,
   187→			Aggregate: true,
   188→			Type:      &CoreString,
   189→			TypeIds:   IntToStringSlice(cores),
   190→			Scope:     scope,
   191→		})
   192→		return results, true
   193→	}
   194→
   195→	// MemoryDomain -> MemoryDomain
   196→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeMemoryDomain && scope == schema.MetricScopeMemoryDomain {
   197→		memDomains, _ := topology.GetMemoryDomainsFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   198→		results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
   199→			Metric:    metric,
   200→			Hostname:  hostname,
   201→			Aggregate: false,
   202→			Type:      &MemoryDomainString,
   203→			TypeIds:   IntToStringSlice(memDomains),
   204→			Scope:     scope,
   205→		})
   206→		return results, true
   207→	}
   208→
   209→	// MemoryDomain -> Socket
   210→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeMemoryDomain && scope == schema.MetricScopeSocket {
   211→		memDomains, _ := topology.GetMemoryDomainsFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   212→		socketToDomains, err := topology.GetMemoryDomainsBySocket(memDomains)
   213→		if err != nil {
   214→			cclog.Errorf("Error mapping memory domains to sockets, return unchanged: %v", err)
   215→			// Rare Error Case -> Still Continue -> Return Empty Slice
   216→			return results, true
   217→		}
   218→
   219→		// Create a query for each socket
   220→		for _, domains := range socketToDomains {
   221→			results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
   222→				Metric:    metric,
   223→				Hostname:  hostname,
   224→				Aggregate: true,
   225→				Type:      &MemoryDomainString,
   226→				TypeIds:   IntToStringSlice(domains),
   227→				Scope:     scope,
   228→			})
   229→		}
   230→		return results, true
   231→	}
   232→
   233→	// MemoryDomain -> Node
   234→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeMemoryDomain && scope == schema.MetricScopeNode {
   235→		memDomains, _ := topology.GetMemoryDomainsFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   236→		results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
   237→			Metric:    metric,
   238→			Hostname:  hostname,
   239→			Aggregate: true,
   240→			Type:      &MemoryDomainString,
   241→			TypeIds:   IntToStringSlice(memDomains),
   242→			Scope:     scope,
   243→		})
   244→		return results, true
   245→	}
   246→
   247→	// Socket -> Socket
   248→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeSocket && scope == schema.MetricScopeSocket {
   249→		sockets, _ := topology.GetSocketsFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   250→		results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
   251→			Metric:    metric,
   252→			Hostname:  hostname,
   253→			Aggregate: false,
   254→			Type:      &SocketString,
   255→			TypeIds:   IntToStringSlice(sockets),
   256→			Scope:     scope,
   257→		})
   258→		return results, true
   259→	}
   260→
   261→	// Socket -> Node
   262→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeSocket && scope == schema.MetricScopeNode {
   263→		sockets, _ := topology.GetSocketsFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   264→		results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
   265→			Metric:    metric,
   266→			Hostname:  hostname,
   267→			Aggregate: true,
   268→			Type:      &SocketString,
   269→			TypeIds:   IntToStringSlice(sockets),
   270→			Scope:     scope,
   271→		})
   272→		return results, true
   273→	}
   274→
   275→	// Node -> Node
   276→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeNode && scope == schema.MetricScopeNode {
   277→		results = append(results, ScopeQueryResult{
   278→			Metric:   metric,
   279→			Hostname: hostname,
   280→			Scope:    scope,
   281→		})
   282→		return results, true
   283→	}
   284→
   285→	// Unhandled Case
   286→	return nil, false
   287→}
   288→
   289→// IntToStringSlice converts a slice of integers to a slice of strings.
   290→// Used to convert hardware thread/core/socket IDs from topology (int) to query TypeIds (string).
   291→// Optimized to reuse a byte buffer for string conversion, reducing allocations.
   292→func IntToStringSlice(is []int) []string {
   293→	if len(is) == 0 {
   294→		return nil
   295→	}
   296→
   297→	ss := make([]string, len(is))
   298→	buf := make([]byte, 0, 16) // Reusable buffer for integer conversion
   299→	for i, x := range is {
   300→		buf = strconv.AppendInt(buf[:0], int64(x), 10)
   301→		ss[i] = string(buf)
   302→	}
   303→	return ss
   304→}
   305→
   306→// ExtractTypeID returns the type ID at the given index from a query's TypeIds slice.
   307→// Returns nil if queryType is nil (no type filtering). Logs a warning and returns nil
   308→// if the index is out of range.
   309→func ExtractTypeID(queryType *string, typeIds []string, ndx int, metric, hostname string) *string {
   310→	if queryType == nil {
   311→		return nil
   312→	}
   313→	if ndx < len(typeIds) {
   314→		id := typeIds[ndx]
   315→		return &id
   316→	}
   317→	cclog.Warnf("TypeIds index out of range: %d with length %d for metric %s on host %s",
   318→		ndx, len(typeIds), metric, hostname)
   319→	return nil
   320→}
   321→
   322→// IsMetricRemovedForSubCluster checks whether a metric is marked as removed
   323→// for the given subcluster in its per-subcluster configuration.
   324→func IsMetricRemovedForSubCluster(mc *schema.MetricConfig, subCluster string) bool {
   325→	for _, scConfig := range mc.SubClusters {
   326→		if scConfig.Name == subCluster && scConfig.Remove {
   327→			return true
   328→		}
   329→	}
   330→	return false
   331→}
   332→
   333→// SanitizeStats replaces NaN values in statistics with 0 to enable JSON marshaling.
   334→// If ANY of avg/min/max is NaN, ALL three are zeroed for consistency.
   335→func SanitizeStats(avg, min, max *schema.Float) {
   336→	if avg.IsNaN() || min.IsNaN() || max.IsNaN() {
   337→		*avg = schema.Float(0)
   338→		*min = schema.Float(0)
   339→		*max = schema.Float(0)
   340→	}
   341→}

     6→// This file implements ingestion of InfluxDB line-protocol metric data received
     7→// over NATS. Each line encodes one metric sample with the following structure:
     8→//
     9→//	<measurement>[,cluster=<c>][,hostname=<h>][,type=<t>][,type-id=<id>][,subtype=<s>][,stype-id=<id>] value=<v> [<timestamp>]
    10→//
    11→// The measurement name identifies the metric (e.g. "cpu_load"). Tags provide
    12→// routing information (cluster, host) and optional sub-device selectors (type,
    13→// subtype). Only one field is expected per line: "value".
    14→//
    15→// After decoding, each sample is:
    16→//  1. Written to the in-memory store via ms.WriteToLevel.
    17→//  2. If the checkpoint format is "wal", also forwarded to the WAL staging
    18→//     goroutine via the WALMessages channel for durable write-ahead logging.
    19→package metricstore
    20→
    21→import (
    22→	"bytes"
    23→	"context"
    24→	"fmt"
    25→	"sync"
    26→	"sync/atomic"
    27→	"time"
    28→
    29→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    30→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/nats"
    31→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    32→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-line-protocol/v2/lineprotocol"
    33→)
    34→
    35→// ReceiveNats subscribes to all configured NATS subjects and feeds incoming
    36→// line-protocol messages into the MemoryStore.
    37→//
    38→// When workers > 1 a pool of goroutines drains a shared channel so that
    39→// multiple messages can be decoded in parallel. With workers == 1 the NATS
    40→// callback decodes inline (no channel overhead, lower latency).
    41→//
    42→// The function blocks until ctx is cancelled and all worker goroutines have
    43→// finished. It returns nil when the NATS client is not configured; callers
    44→// should treat that as a no-op rather than an error.
    45→func ReceiveNats(ms *MemoryStore,
    46→	workers int,
    47→	ctx context.Context,
    48→) error {
    49→	nc := nats.GetClient()
    50→
    51→	if nc == nil {
    52→		cclog.Warn("NATS client not initialized")
    53→		return nil
    54→	}
    55→
    56→	var wg sync.WaitGroup
    57→	msgs := make(chan []byte, max(workers*256, 8192))
    58→
    59→	for _, sc := range *Keys.Subscriptions {
    60→		clusterTag := sc.ClusterTag
    61→		if workers > 1 {
    62→			wg.Add(workers)
    63→
    64→			for range workers {
    65→				go func() {
    66→					defer wg.Done()
    67→					for m := range msgs {
    68→						dec := lineprotocol.NewDecoderWithBytes(m)
    69→						if err := DecodeLine(dec, ms, clusterTag); err != nil {
    70→							cclog.Errorf("error: %s", err.Error())
    71→						}
    72→					}
    73→				}()
    74→			}
    75→
    76→			nc.Subscribe(sc.SubscribeTo, func(subject string, data []byte) {
    77→				select {
    78→				case msgs <- data:
    79→				case <-ctx.Done():
    80→				}
    81→			})
    82→		} else {
    83→			nc.Subscribe(sc.SubscribeTo, func(subject string, data []byte) {
    84→				dec := lineprotocol.NewDecoderWithBytes(data)
    85→				if err := DecodeLine(dec, ms, clusterTag); err != nil {
    86→					cclog.Errorf("error: %s", err.Error())
    87→				}
    88→			})
    89→		}
    90→		cclog.Infof("NATS subscription to '%s' established", sc.SubscribeTo)
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	go func() {
    94→		<-ctx.Done()
    95→		close(msgs)
    96→	}()
    97→
    98→	wg.Wait()
    99→
   100→	return nil
   101→}
   102→
   103→// reorder prepends prefix to buf in-place when buf has enough spare capacity,
   104→// avoiding an allocation. Falls back to a regular append otherwise.
   105→//
   106→// It is used to assemble the "type<type-id>" and "subtype<stype-id>" selector
   107→// strings when the type tag arrives before the type-id tag in the line, so the
   108→// two byte slices need to be concatenated in tag-declaration order regardless
   109→// of wire order.
   110→func reorder(buf, prefix []byte) []byte {
   111→	n := len(prefix)
   112→	m := len(buf)
   113→	if cap(buf) < m+n {
   114→		return append(prefix[:n:n], buf...)
   115→	} else {
   116→		buf = buf[:n+m]
   117→		for i := m - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
   118→			buf[i+n] = buf[i]
   119→		}
   120→		for i := range n {
   121→			buf[i] = prefix[i]
   122→		}
   123→		return buf
   124→	}
   125→}
   126→
   127→// walDropped counts WAL messages dropped due to a full channel.
   128→// Logged periodically; not critical since binary snapshots capture the data.
   129→var walDropped atomic.Int64
   130→
   131→// decodeState holds the per-call scratch buffers used by DecodeLine.
   132→// Instances are recycled via decodeStatePool to avoid repeated allocations
   133→// during high-throughput ingestion.
   134→type decodeState struct {
   135→	// metricBuf holds a copy of the current measurement name (line-protocol
   136→	// measurement field). Copied because dec.Measurement() returns a slice
   137→	// that is invalidated by the next decoder call.
   138→	metricBuf []byte
   139→
   140→	// selector is the sub-device path passed to WriteToLevel and WALMessage
   141→	// (e.g. ["socket0"] or ["socket0", "memctrl1"]). Reused across lines.
   142→	selector []string
   143→
   144→	// typeBuf accumulates the concatenated "type"+"type-id" tag value for the
   145→	// current line. Reset at the start of each line's tag-decode loop.
   146→	typeBuf []byte
   147→
   148→	// subTypeBuf accumulates the concatenated "subtype"+"stype-id" tag value.
   149→	// Reset at the start of each line's tag-decode loop.
   150→	subTypeBuf []byte
   151→
   152→	// prevTypeBytes / prevTypeStr cache the last seen typeBuf content and its
   153→	// string conversion. Because consecutive lines in a batch typically address
   154→	// the same sub-device, the cache hit rate is very high and avoids
   155→	// repeated []byte→string allocations.
   156→	prevTypeBytes []byte
   157→	prevTypeStr   string
   158→
   159→	// prevSubTypeBytes / prevSubTypeStr are the same cache for the subtype.
   160→	prevSubTypeBytes []byte
   161→	prevSubTypeStr   string
   162→}
   163→
   164→// decodeStatePool recycles decodeState values across DecodeLine calls to
   165→// reduce GC pressure during sustained metric ingestion.
   166→var decodeStatePool = sync.Pool{
   167→	New: func() any {
   168→		return &decodeState{
   169→			metricBuf:  make([]byte, 0, 16),
   170→			selector:   make([]string, 0, 4),
   171→			typeBuf:    make([]byte, 0, 16),
   172→			subTypeBuf: make([]byte, 0, 16),
   173→		}
   174→	},
   175→}
   176→
   177→// DecodeLine reads all lines from dec (InfluxDB line-protocol) and writes each
   178→// decoded metric sample into ms.
   179→//
   180→// clusterDefault is used as the cluster name for lines that do not carry a
   181→// "cluster" tag. Callers typically supply the ClusterTag value from the NATS
   182→// subscription configuration.
   183→//
   184→// Performance notes:
   185→//   - A decodeState is obtained from decodeStatePool to reuse scratch buffers.
   186→//   - The Level pointer (host-level node in the metric tree) is cached across
   187→//     consecutive lines that share the same cluster+host pair to avoid
   188→//     repeated lock acquisitions on the root and cluster levels.
   189→//   - []byte→string conversions for type/subtype selectors are cached via
   190→//     prevType*/prevSubType* fields because batches typically repeat the same
   191→//     sub-device identifiers.
   192→//   - Timestamp parsing tries Second precision first; if that fails it retries
   193→//     Millisecond, Microsecond, and Nanosecond in turn. A missing timestamp
   194→//     falls back to time.Now().
   195→//
   196→// When the checkpoint format is "wal" each successfully decoded sample is also
   197→// sent to WALMessages so the WAL staging goroutine can persist it durably
   198→// before the next binary snapshot.
   199→func DecodeLine(dec *lineprotocol.Decoder,
   200→	ms *MemoryStore,
   201→	clusterDefault string,
   202→) error {
   203→	// Reduce allocations in loop:
   204→	t := time.Now()
   205→	metric := Metric{}
   206→	st := decodeStatePool.Get().(*decodeState)
   207→	defer decodeStatePool.Put(st)
   208→
   209→	// Optimize for the case where all lines in a "batch" are about the same
   210→	// cluster and host. By using `WriteToLevel` (level = host), we do not need
   211→	// to take the root- and cluster-level lock as often.
   212→	var lvl *Level = nil
   213→	prevCluster, prevHost := "", ""
   214→
   215→	var ok bool
   216→	for dec.Next() {
   217→		rawmeasurement, err := dec.Measurement()
   218→		if err != nil {
   219→			return err
   220→		}
   221→

   223→		// invalidate the returned slice.
